strengths: Completed the full circular cut in 120 seconds, well under the 300-second benchmark
Successfully executed all required phases including proper traction setup and circle release
Maintained consistent cutting motion throughout the majority of the circle
weaknesses: Two visible deviations from the marked circle line during the cutting sequence
Inconsistent precision in maintaining exact line adherence during curved sections

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, pausing between each quarter to verify line adherence before proceeding.

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle and maintain optimal cutting geometry throughout the circle.

progression_note: At the advanced level, your speed is excellent but precision refinements are needed to eliminate line deviations and achieve consistent benchmark performance with minimal deductions.
